title: End of Evangelion re release
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion re-release             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Jul 02, 2005 9:21 pm It looks like Manga Entertainment's re-releasing Death and Rebirth/End of Evangelion at the end of July. Judging by the description on their website, this is just a repackaging of their old DVD masters... [url]http://www.manga.com/index.php?id=77[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Sat Jul 02, 2005 9:28 pm Yep. I'm not trying to be rude, but this has been posted before. There's another thread right here. We'll never get to see the glory that is the renewal versions unless Mangle Entertainment lose that license. It sucks. :(

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Jul 02, 2005 9:48 pm The box set referenced in that thread is not region 1 and has already been released. This is the one that Monkey mentioned had been delayed and has a different design.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Sat Jul 02, 2005 9:59 pm drinian wrote: The box set referenced in that thread is not region 1 and has already been released. This is the one that Monkey mentioned had been delayed and has a different design.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Sun Jul 03, 2005 3:49 am Ok I'm just wondering something. The end of evangelion that I already own looks as good, if not better than the platinum editions. now what's the point in releasing a renewed version??

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Bud » Sun Jul 03, 2005 8:23 am rob_of_the_genesis wrote: Ok I'm just wondering something. The end of evangelion that I already own looks as good, if not better than the platinum editions. now what's the point in releasing a renewed version??

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Jul 03, 2005 2:00 pm Mr. Bud wrote: rob_of_the_genesis wrote: Ok I'm just wondering something. The end of evangelion that I already own looks as good, if not better than the platinum editions. now what's the point in releasing a renewed version??

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Sun Jul 03, 2005 2:33 pm ah so in the end it's all about the money basically.. figures.

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Sun Jul 03, 2005 6:29 pm Okay, the renewal of eva versions consist of death (true)^2 and much improved color and sound. Manga has the shitty old death and rebirth, plus the picture is quite suckie and the sound ultimatelly blows... only 5.1 english for d&r and 5.1 and 6.1 english and Japanese for eoe. What were they thinking? The two go together like shit and a toilet. I have the renewal DTS version of eoe and the manga dvds so I know. That is the difference.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Bud » Sun Jul 03, 2005 8:53 pm rip3mwk wrote: Okay, the renewal of eva versions consist of death (true)^2 and much improved color and sound. Manga has the shitty old death and rebirth, plus the picture is quite suckie and the sound ultimatelly blows... only 5.1 english for d&r and 5.1 and 6.1 english and Japanese for eoe. What were they thinking? The two go together like shit and a toilet. I have the renewal DTS version of eoe and the manga dvds so I know. That is the difference.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Sun Jul 03, 2005 10:02 pm True dat!

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Jul 04, 2005 4:21 am Mr. Bud wrote: rip3mwk wrote: Okay, the renewal of eva versions consist of death (true)^2 and much improved color and sound. Manga has the shitty old death and rebirth, plus the picture is quite suckie and the sound ultimatelly blows... only 5.1 english for d&r and 5.1 and 6.1 english and Japanese for eoe. What were they thinking? The two go together like shit and a toilet. I have the renewal DTS version of eoe and the manga dvds so I know. That is the difference.

[]:             Quote Post by RuaDragon » Mon Jul 04, 2005 6:59 am [quote="Mr. Bud] Oh well, Death & Rebirth is lame anyway so...[/quote] TAKE THAT BACK HEATHEN!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Bud » Mon Jul 04, 2005 9:05 am A really long clip show with half of EoE slapped on the end of it...Great.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Jul 04, 2005 1:09 pm Mr. Bud wrote: A really long clip show with half of EoE slapped on the end of it...Great.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Mon Jul 04, 2005 1:25 pm Yeah they put Rebirth on to give the people that went to see Death and Rebirth in theaters more of a reason to want to see End of Evangelion. In the theater that was a great idea because then you don't just go to the theater and see chips from the episodes you've already seen. but when it makes it's way to dvd it becomes usless with end of Evangelion out. and I don't hate DeathandRebirth it's just pointless for ME personally because I already have all 7 platinums with the directors cuts on them and end of evangelion. for somebody who doesn't have the directors cuts it's actually awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Ataru » Tue Jul 19, 2005 12:03 pm I love Death & Rebirth because it condenses almost the entire series down into a tightly edited assualt on your senses. It's brutal. For example, there's no "Asuka is annoying for 6-9 hours then you find out why", they just smash you with her mother revelation right at the start, etc. etc. It's really unpleasant. Death & Rebirth is also great because if you have friends who like crazy movies but wouldn't be receptive to watching all of Eva. On my first viewings I thought the series was pretty irritating until half way through, then fell in love with it during the final third. Death & Rebirth is a good way to introduce the show to people who wouldn't bother watching it if they just saw the first few episodes. Does it "spoil" them? Not really, since it's unlikely they'd ever watch the show anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Tue Jul 19, 2005 6:36 pm I like death. That still doesn't give me a reason for buying REBIRTH with it...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 26, 2005 4:26 pm Rebirth is pointless, yes. But Death is not. End of story.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Jul 26, 2005 4:35 pm At least this will provide something to stick into that big Platinum box. I'm really not attached to those cardboard jackets at all.
